_comment: Curated illustrative examples for the derivatives use-case core concepts. Real-world derivatives instances (general domain knowledge), not FIBO axioms. Applied by to_okf when FIBO supplies none; provenance curated.

examples.title: derivative instrument
examples: An interest-rate swap or an equity call option — a contract whose value derives from an underlying.

examples.title: credit derivative
examples: A credit default swap that transfers the credit risk of a bond issuer.

examples.title: rate-based derivative
examples: An interest-rate swap referencing SOFR.

examples.title: currency derivative
examples: A EUR/USD forward that locks in an exchange rate.

examples.title: commodity derivative
examples: A crude-oil futures contract on WTI.

examples.title: derivative terms
examples: The strike, expiry, and notional recorded in an option's contract terms.

examples.title: underlier
examples: The S&P 500 index underlying an index option.

examples.title: cashflow formula
examples: The formula 'notional x (SOFR + 0.5%)' for a swap's floating leg.

examples.title: option
examples: A contract giving the right to buy 100 Apple shares at $200 by December.

examples.title: call option
examples: The right to buy 100 Apple shares at a $200 strike by expiry.

examples.title: put option
examples: The right to sell 100 Apple shares at a $180 strike by expiry.

examples.title: strike price
examples: The $200 strike at which an Apple call may be exercised.

examples.title: moneyness
examples: An in-the-money call whose $180 strike is below the $200 spot price.

examples.title: covered call
examples: Selling a call on shares you already own to earn the premium.

examples.title: bond option
examples: An option to buy a 10-year Treasury note at a set price.

examples.title: currency option
examples: An option to buy EUR against USD at 1.10.

examples.title: equity option
examples: A listed call option on Tesla shares.

examples.title: swaption
examples: An option to enter a 5-year interest-rate swap one year from now.

examples.title: Asian option
examples: An option whose payoff uses the average price of oil over the month.

examples.title: barrier option
examples: A call that only activates if the underlying rises above $250.

examples.title: knock-in option
examples: A barrier option that starts existing once the price touches $250.

examples.title: knock-out option
examples: A barrier option that expires worthless if the price hits $150.

examples.title: digital option
examples: An option paying a fixed $100 if the stock ends above $200, else nothing.

examples.title: lookback option
examples: An option whose payoff uses the most favourable price over its life.

examples.title: compound option
examples: An option to buy another option (an 'option on an option').

examples.title: future
examples: A standardized exchange-traded contract to buy oil at a set price next month.

examples.title: forward
examples: A privately negotiated contract to buy wheat at $6/bushel in three months.

examples.title: bond future
examples: A CME 10-year US Treasury note futures contract.

examples.title: index future
examples: An E-mini S&P 500 futures contract.

examples.title: equity future
examples: A single-stock future on a listed company's shares.

examples.title: currency future
examples: A CME EUR/USD futures contract.

examples.title: equity forward
examples: A forward to buy 1,000 shares in six months at a set price.

examples.title: forward rate agreement
examples: An agreement to fix a 3-month interest rate starting in six months.

examples.title: currency forward
examples: A 90-day forward to buy GBP1m for dollars at 1.27.

examples.title: swap
examples: A contract in which two parties exchange fixed for floating interest payments.

examples.title: swap leg
examples: The fixed-rate side of an interest-rate swap.

examples.title: fixed leg
examples: The leg paying a fixed 3% on the notional.

examples.title: floating leg
examples: The leg paying SOFR + 0.4% on the notional.

examples.title: interest rate swap
examples: Exchanging fixed 3% for floating SOFR on a $10m notional.

examples.title: currency swap
examples: Exchanging USD principal and interest for EUR over five years.

examples.title: basis swap
examples: A swap exchanging SOFR-linked for Fed-Funds-linked payments.

examples.title: overnight index swap
examples: A swap whose floating leg compounds an overnight rate such as SOFR.

examples.title: total return swap
examples: A swap paying the total return of a bond in exchange for a financing rate.

examples.title: equity swap
examples: A swap paying the return of the S&P 500 versus a floating rate.

examples.title: commodity swap
examples: A swap exchanging a fixed price for the floating price of crude oil.

examples.title: credit default swap
examples: A CDS where the buyer pays a premium for protection against a bond issuer's default.

examples.title: triggering event
examples: A bond issuer's bankruptcy that triggers a CDS payout.

examples.title: credit event notice
examples: The formal notice that a reference entity has defaulted.

examples.title: deliverable asset
examples: A defaulted bond delivered to settle a physically-settled CDS.

examples.title: loan credit default swap
examples: A CDS whose reference obligation is a syndicated loan.

examples.title: credit default swap index
examples: The CDX North America Investment Grade index of CDS.

examples.title: credit protection terms
examples: The terms defining which credit events trigger a CDS payout.

examples.title: counterparty
examples: The bank on the other side of your interest-rate swap.

examples.title: master agreement
examples: An ISDA Master Agreement governing all trades between two parties.

examples.title: derivative master agreement
examples: The ISDA Master Agreement covering a pair's OTC derivatives.

examples.title: swap dealer
examples: A bank registered as a swap dealer that makes markets in swaps.

examples.title: clearing house
examples: A central counterparty such as LCH that clears interest-rate swaps.

examples.title: paying party
examples: The party obligated to make the fixed payments on a swap leg.

examples.title: receiving counterparty
examples: The party that receives the floating payments on a swap leg.

examples.title: structured product
examples: A capital-protected note whose return is linked to an equity index.
